data.title: US stocks plunged rapidly, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average falling 0.63%.
data.description: According to market news: U.S. stocks plunged rapidly, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average down 0.63%, the S&P 500 down 0.69%, and the Nasdaq Composite down 0.79%.

data.title: U.S. short-term interest rate futures rose as traders increased bets on a Fed rate cut.
data.description: U.S. short-term interest rate futures rose as traders increased their bets on a Federal Reserve rate cut. In addition, the S&P 500 fell more than 1%, its biggest drop since September 2.

data.title: MetaMask plans to launch multi-chain accounts by the end of October
data.description: MetaMask announced on the X platform that it will launch multi-chain accounts at the end of October. This will allow users to group and manage EVM and non-EVM addresses, and enable transactions and tracking of account assets between the two networks. All EVM accounts on MetaMask will retain their names and addresses. MetaMask's multi-chain accounts will reportedly support other networks, including Bitcoin, Tron, and Monad.

data.title: Spot gold rose by about $20 in the short term, breaking through $4010/ounce
data.description: Spot gold rose by about $20 in the short term, breaking through $4,010 per ounce, up 0.86% on the day. (Jinshi)

data.title: The CBOE Volatility Index hits a more than one-month high
data.description: The Chicago Board Options Exchange Volatility Index hit a high of more than one month, and was last up 1.99 points at 18.42.

data.title: Trump administration indicts New York’s chief crypto regulation enforcer
data.description:
New York Attorney General Letitia James is one of the few Democrats who have had the guts to cross swords with Donald Trump, but she is already paying the price. According to reports, James was indicted Thursday by a federal grand jury in Virginia, and this comes not long after Trump publicly asked his Justice […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/trump-admin-indicts-new-york-crypto-enforcer/

data.title: The short-term decline of the US dollar index DXY expanded to more than 15 points and is now at 99.09
data.description: The US dollar index (DXY) saw its short-term decline extend by over 15 points, currently trading at 99.09. Non-US currencies generally rose, with the euro/USD pair rising nearly 20 points to 1.1600, the British pound/GBP/USD pair rising nearly 40 points to 1.3335, and the dollar/yen pair falling around 40 points to 152.06. European stocks fell rapidly, with the German DAX, French CAC 40, and Italian FTSE MIB all falling by over 1%. (Jinshi)

data.title: In the past hour, the entire network has exploded $100 million, mainly due to the collapse of long orders.
data.description: Coinglass data shows that in the past hour, the entire network’s liquidation reached US$100 million, with long positions liquidated US$96.1308 million and short positions liquidated US$4.3593 million.

data.title: Norwegian officials, who are responsible for awarding the Nobel Peace Prize, are investigating unusual betting practices at Polymarket.
data.description: Norwegian officials, responsible for awarding the Nobel Peace Prize, are investigating unusual betting activity on Polymarket. According to information on the Polymarket website, a surge in bets on Venezuelan opposition leader Maria Corina Machado suggests a potential information leak. Sources say three Polymarket accounts, primarily betting on Machado, profited a total of approximately $90,000. (Bloomberg)

data.title: Hargreaves Lansdown, the UK's largest retail investment platform, has warned clients to stay away from
data.description: Hargreaves Lansdown, the UK's largest retail investment platform with approximately $225 billion in assets under management, issued a surprisingly stern warning to its clients: Stay away from Bitcoin. The notice stated that Bitcoin has no intrinsic value and should not be included in personal savings or retirement plans. (Fortune)
